photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: an underground nuclear waste facility suffers a critical breach, triggering a massive regional evacuation
a revolutionary new form of renewable energy is discovered
megalodon shark sightings off the coast
a new form of transportation is unveiled, completely revolutionizing travel
a small island nation becomes the first to achieve 100% renewable energy
a new form of clean energy is discovered that is cheaper than coal

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a major natural disaster, such as a hurricane or earthquake, that leaves thousands of people displaced
a regional water shortage leads to strict conservation measures and dried-up municipal reservoirs
a major world crisis, such as the covid-19 pandemic or a global economic recession
a major international airport suffers a system-wide it failure, grounding hundreds of commercial flights
a major breakthrough in space exploration, such as the discovery of a new planet or the launch of a new mission to the moon
a major scientific breakthrough, such as a new form of renewable energy or a new vaccine

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a new scientific discovery: scientists discover a new species of dinosaur, providing new insights into the prehistoric world and sparking excitement among paleontologists and the public alike
a mass shooting or act of domestic terrorism: a gunman opens fire at a crowded concert in las vegas, killing dozens and injuring hundreds more
a political protest: demonstrators gather outside the white house to protest the government's immigration policies, calling for greater protection for undocumented immigrants and refugees
a massive wildfire: a fire sweeps through the australian outback, destroying homes and forcing thousands of residents to evacuate
a historic space launch: the world's most powerful rocket lifts off from cape canaveral, carrying a new space telescope to study the early universe
a humanitarian crisis: a civil war in yemen leads to a widespread famine and humanitarian crisis, with millions of people facing starvation and disease

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: aerial photography: providing a unique and expansive view of the protests and the surrounding environment
dramatic silhouette photography: framing protesters against a bright light source, like the setting sun or police headlights, to create powerful dark outlines
underwater and rain-spattered photography: shooting through wet lenses or during heavy downpours to capture the grueling physical conditions of outdoor protests
action photography: capturing the movement and energy of the protesters in action
analog film photography: using traditional 35mm or medium format film to create a raw, grain-textured aesthetic with natural color tones
drone photography: capturing birds-eye-view angles of massive crowds to illustrate the sheer scale and layout of public demonstrations

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: architectural and urban preservation, documenting historic landmarks threatened by development

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: A woman is playing a soft melody on his lute while sitting by the fountain in the castle courtyard.
A dry rainbow rose is coming back to life.
Camera zoom out. A couple walking along the beach as the sun sets over the ocean.
The person's cheeks flushed with embarrassment as he tripped in public.
A person opens a book and turns it upside-down and characters from the book begin to fall out of it.
